content.problemStatement:
Chefland has 777 days in a week. Chef is very conscious about his work done during the week.

There are two ways he can spend his energy during the week. The first way is to do xxx units of work every day and the second way is to do yyy (>x\gt x>x) units of work for the first ddd (<7\lt 7<7) days and to do zzz (<x\lt x<x) units of work thereafter since he will get tired of working more in the initial few days.

Find the maximum amount of work he can do during the week if he is free to choose either of the two strategies.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers d, x, y, z.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum total work Chef can do during the 7‑day week.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case compute both strategies and take the larger value. The results are 14, 19 and 14 respectively.

hidden.label: edge case where second strategy is better

solution.approach:
For each test case compute the total work for the two possible strategies:
1. Constant work: total1 = 7 * x.
2. Front‑loaded work: total2 = d * y + (7 - d) * z.
The answer is max(total1, total2). All operations fit in 64‑bit signed integers.
The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        d = int(next(it)); x = int(next(it)); y = int(next(it)); z = int(next(it))
        total1 = 7 * x
        total2 = d * y + (7 - d) * z
        out.append(str(max(total1, total2)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
